title: poa flexuosa subsp. fernaldiana
intro: Poa flexuosa subsp. fernaldiana, also known as Fernald's blue grass or Fernald's poa, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Poa flexuosa subsp. fernaldiana include soothing digestive issues, relieving respiratory problems, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
